symptoms.symptom_name: Lapse in PrEP supply immediately before a planned high-risk exposure (condomless receptive anal intercourse)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: PrEP continuation / urgent bridging supply required
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An adherence gap directly before high-risk exposure is exactly the situation where a bridging supply is time-critical to maintain protective drug levels.

symptoms.symptom_name: Strict prior daily adherence to tenofovir/emtricitabine until today

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: PrEP continuation / urgent bridging supply required
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continuous prior adherence supports established protective coverage and makes breakthrough infection during the covered period unlikely.
Reliable prior adherence up to today argues against a covered-period seroconversion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of acute retroviral syndrome symptoms (no fever, myalgia, sore throat, rash or lymphadenopathy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of the acute retroviral syndrome constellation makes occult acute HIV unlikely, though updated testing is still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent (approximately 4 weeks ago) negative fourth-generation HIV test and normal eGFR

investigation_details.investigation_name: Fourth-generation HIV antigen/antibody test and serum creatinine/eGFR
investigation_details.expected_result: HIV Ag/Ab negative; eGFR within normal range (updated result required before ongoing prescribing)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without updated HIV and renal results, ongoing tenofovir-based PrEP cannot be safely confirmed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: PrEP continuation / urgent bridging supply required
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Documented recent negative HIV status and normal renal function meet the prerequisites for safe continuation.
A recent normal eGFR argues against renal impairment as a barrier to continuation.

symptoms.symptom_name: No genitourinary symptoms and a recent negative STI screen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No dysuria, discharge or ulceration and a recent clear screen make a current concurrent STI less likely, though PrEP does not treat STIs and ongoing screening is required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured stable vital signs (BP 125/80, HR 88, afebrile 36.7, RR 16, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: PrEP continuation / urgent bridging supply required
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective haemodynamic and febrile stability confirm no acute illness — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers wellness from the patient's report, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of lymphadenopathy, pharyngeal erythema or maculopapular rash on physical examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Examined absence of seroconversion signs — clinician-only; the AI can only rely on reported symptoms and video-observable appearance.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Fourth-generation HIV antigen/antibody test (updated, at prescriber visit)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms ongoing HIV-negative status before continuing dual-agent PrEP; guards against continuing through occult acute infection.
Renal function must be adequate for safe tenofovir disoproxil prescribing and monitored periodically.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full STI screen (NAAT for chlamydia/gonorrhoea, syphilis serology)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Part of routine 3-monthly PrEP monitoring; PrEP does not protect against other STIs.
